$AAPL Apparently they are still relying on Tim Cook. So while it is good in the short term it doesn’t speak well for succession and John Ternus. Broadcom is a winner because everyone uses them to help with chip designs. It’s very interesting since Apple does in-house chip design but in this case they chose an outside company. The tea leaves say it must not be a CPU like M and A. These things make me wonder if Tim Cook was forced out. Very little visible transition is occurring. Almost like Cook is sending a message that he’s still in charge. The China deal is an example of this too.  But even there, it runs the risk of being reversed. How will the Trump Administration view Apple computers built with Chinese made chips from CXMT? Tim Cook apparently does not want John Ternus to get credit for his work. Ternus’ entry is delayed until Cook is really out, but even then he can question him at the board. Will we see sonething like what happened at Disney when Bob Iger stepped down?!
